identitySentence: Little & Logue, PLLC is a Flower Mound family law firm founded in 2023 by Thomas Little and James Logue, guiding Denton County clients through divorce, custody, adoption, mediation, and wills and power of attorney matters.

concept.headline: Facing a family law decision in Denton County? Start the conversation with Little & Logue, PLLC.
concept.subheadline: Divorce, custody, and adoption questions rarely arrive at a convenient hour. Share your situation through our secure form and receive a prompt, personal response with real consultation times, so you know exactly what happens next.
credibilityPoints: Co-founded in 2023 by family law attorneys Thomas Little and James Logue
James Logue has practiced family law since 2012 and was named to The Best Lawyers in America from 2021 to 2023
Thomas Little is an Army veteran, trained mediator, and Super Lawyers Rising Star from 2019 to 2025
2024 Best of Denton County honoree in both the family law and wills and estate planning categories

sections.title: Practice focus
sections.body: Little & Logue, PLLC concentrates on family law: divorce and uncontested divorce, child custody and support, adoption, alimony, and mediation, along with fathers', mothers', and grandparents' rights, paternity, restraining orders, visitation, and modifications. The firm also helps with wills and power of attorney, so families can take care of closely related planning in one place. Mediation services are available for those who prefer to resolve disputes outside the courtroom.

sections.title: Denton roots
sections.body: From its office at 4305 Windsor Centre Trail, Suite 100 in Flower Mound, the firm practices regularly in the Denton County Family Courts. That means working familiarity with local judges, local procedures, and the mediation norms that shape how Denton County family cases actually move.

sections.title: What to expect
sections.body: Reach out by phone at (940) 204-5535 or through the online form, and the firm will ask straightforward questions about your situation, your children if custody is involved, and the outcome you hope for. Virtual appointments are available, and the first conversation is about understanding your matter honestly, not pressuring you into anything.

outreachDraft.subject: Proof matters more than promises: 15 days, 1 demo, and a Lead Capturer that can pay for itself before you ever have to.
outreachDraft.body:
DRAFT — NOT SENT. Review and authorize before any outreach.

Little & Logue, PLLC
4305 Windsor Centre Trail, Suite 100
Flower Mound, TX 75028

Dear Thomas Little and James Logue,

When Best of Denton County named Little & Logue a 2024 honoree in both its family law firm and wills and estate planning law firm categories, barely a year after you co-founded the firm in 2023, it confirmed something your clients likely already knew: families in Denton County trust you with their hardest moments.

Your website already does a great deal right. It is modern, mobile-friendly, speaks plainly about divorce, custody, and mediation, and gives visitors a clear path to call (940) 204-5535 or send a contact form. What it cannot yet do is answer at the moment a potential client actually reaches out. Family law inquiries rarely arrive between nine and five. A parent worried about a custody order at 10 p.m., or a spouse finally ready to ask about divorce on a Sunday, sends your form into a quiet inbox and waits. Many will send the same message to two or three other firms that same night. The firm that responds first with something genuinely useful is usually the one that gets the consultation.

That is the entire job of the Lead Capturer we have built for Little & Logue. It is a custom contact form for your site, introduced by an identity sentence written specifically for your firm: "Little & Logue, PLLC is a Flower Mound family law firm founded in 2023 by Thomas Little and James Logue, guiding Denton County clients through divorce, custody, adoption, mediation, and wills and power of attorney matters."

Here is what it does:

1. Every inquiry is emailed directly to the firm's inbox, with the sender's address set as Reply-To so you can respond personally in one step.
2. The potential client automatically receives a response specific to their message. A custody question gets a custody-focused reply; a wills question gets a wills-focused reply, each written for your practice areas.
3. That response presents your genuinely available consultation times.
4. When the person selects a time, a $50 reservation deposit confirms the appointment, so your calendar holds committed consultations rather than maybes.

No one on your staff has to do anything for steps 2 through 4 to happen, and every message still lands in your inbox for you to own from there.

We would rather prove this on your own practice than persuade you with adjectives. We are offering Little & Logue a 15-day proof period: we build the complete experience, you test it end to end, and you judge it on evidence instead of promises.

How to test your Lead Capturer (15 minutes):

1. Submit a clearly labeled TEST inquiry using a customer email you control.
2. Do not enter confidential client information.
3. Confirm the firm inbox receives a real email.
4. Confirm the notification contains every displayed form field.
5. Confirm the subject identifies the firm and the likely matter type.
6. Confirm Reply-To contains the submitted customer email.
7. Confirm the system automatically generates a response specific to the TEST inquiry.
8. Confirm the response is sent to the customer email without any staff action.
9. Confirm the customer actually receives it.
10. Confirm it addresses the actual inquiry topic.
11. Confirm genuine available consultation times are presented.
12. Select a time.
13. Confirm the $50 reservation requirement appears.
14. Confirm the preparation list fits the matter.
15. Confirm the prohibited-item list includes legal documents.
16. In a payment sandbox, confirm the appointment locks only after a verified $50 payment event.

If any step fails, the test fails — contact us.

We will not claim the Lead Capturer will add a specific dollar figure to your revenue; practices differ too much for that to be an honest promise. We will say that it answers every inquiry immediately, with your real availability, at the hour the inquiry actually arrives, and that the 15-day test above lets you verify each of those claims yourself before spending anything.

Respectfully,

The IntakeBrief Team
[Physical mailing address: provided before any send]
If you would prefer not to hear from us again, reply with 'unsubscribe' and we will remove you immediately.
